task: Add the next Pro Rust product-core slice: a keyless execution intent preview that derives a rejected execution-intent envelope from an existing preview job handoff and worker-lease boundary. Expose one local API endpoint, render one graph-first web panel, and synchronize Pro docs/evidence. This remains preview-only and must not add sessions, passwords, JWT validation, external auth, credential storage, provider calls, quota or billing mutation, worker execution, queue persistence, package publishing, npm dependencies, or OSS runtime changes.
securityRequirements: Mention auth, secrets, permissions, and sensitive-data handling explicitly.
Do not accept or store passwords, tokens, provider secrets, or API keys.
Execution intent preview must deny live/provider/worker/result-write paths until hosted auth, vault-handle, quota-reservation, worker-lease, and idempotent-commit gates exist.
dependencyRequirements: Mention new or upgraded packages, lockfile changes, and dependency impact explicitly.
performanceRequirements: Mention performance, latency, or load impact in evidence.
understandingRequirements: Explain why this intent preview derives from existing handoff and worker-lease vocabulary instead of enabling execution or storing a queue intent.
continuityRequirements: Reuse the existing execution queue, handoff preview, worker lease boundary, Axum endpoint style, and compact web panel patterns.
patternSummary: Reuse the existing Pro queue handoff payload, worker lease boundary payload, API route handlers, web fetchJson helper, and preview-only boundary panels.
smallestViableChange: Add a deterministic execution intent preview payload that combines a queued job handoff preview with worker lease rules and reports intent creation denied until hosted prerequisites are satisfied.
assumptions: The Pro runtime remains local preview-only.
Real provider/search execution is still intentionally blocked.
Credential values are never accepted, displayed, stored, or returned by this intent preview.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue tests prove the intent preview denies intent creation and contains handoff plus worker-lease blockers.
API tests prove the endpoint returns a server-computed execution intent preview payload and no secret-shaped fields.
Web tests prove the graph-first shell contains the intent preview panel, endpoint path, and renderer.
Docs and evidence explain that this is not real provider execution, credential access, quota mutation, or auth enforcement.
expectedBehaviorChanges: Pro API exposes a keyless execution intent preview endpoint per preview job.
Pro web shell renders whether a queued job can create a future hosted execution intent and which hosted prerequisites block it.
riskJustification: This is a keyless local execution intent preview that composes the existing denied handoff and worker-lease boundary. It prepares an explicit intent envelope without enabling provider calls, credential reads, quota reservations, queue persistence, worker execution, or result writes.
